---
title: "RoPE Certifier"
---

This lesson is the first standalone Circle Calculus AI contract meant for an ML engineer who does not know Lean.

The question is narrow:

```text
Given a RoPE-like position configuration, can this declared phase bank distinguish positions in the inspected context?
```

The formal answer starts with an exact discretized model. Each channel has an integer period, and a position is represented by its residue modulo that period. The real-phase program also has theorem-backed finite-margin examples: a rational/discretized `1/4099` preset, a bounded standard-RoPE channel-0 interval seed, and a conditional bank bridge when that channel is present. Model-scale real-valued RoPE phases are still scanned numerically, and that margin report is not a formal proof.

::: {.lesson-goal}

## Goal

Learn how the certifier turns RoPE position indistinguishability into a finite divisibility check, then inspect a configuration with the browser widget or Python CLI.

:::

## The Contract

For one integer-period channel:

```text
phase(period, position) = position mod period
```

For a finite bank of periods, two ordered positions collide exactly when every declared period divides the position gap:

```text
bank_collision(left, right)
iff
forall period in periods:
  period divides (right - left)
```

That is the main theorem-backed contract. The Python and browser certifiers compute the common collision gap implied by the declared integer periods. If that gap is at least the context length, no unequal pair inside the inspected context collides in every discrete channel.

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="rope_certifier" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0024" data-dictionary-ids="COMMON-0076 COMMON-0077"></div>

::: {.warning-box}
The widget output is an explanation and executable check. It is not proof by itself. The proof source is the Lean declaration named by the theorem card.
:::

## Python CLI

Use the public certifier from the repository root:

```bash
python scripts/rope_certify.py --head-dim 128 --base 10000 --context 32768 --tolerance 1e-6
```

Named public-safe presets:

```bash
python scripts/rope_certify.py --preset llama_style_10000_4k
python scripts/rope_certify.py --preset llama_style_10000_128k
python scripts/rope_certify.py --preset llama_style_500000_128k
```

Diagnostic exact-discrete failure presets:

```bash
python scripts/rope_certify.py --preset diagnostic_single_channel_10000_20
python scripts/rope_certify.py --preset diagnostic_two_channel_36_128
python scripts/rope_certify.py --preset diagnostic_prefix_pass_4_128
python scripts/rope_certify.py --preset diagnostic_shared_factor_25_64
```

For machine-readable output:

```bash
python scripts/rope_certify.py --preset llama_style_10000_4k --format json
```

The JSON report includes theorem ids, Lean declarations, assumptions, exact discrete pass/fail, bounded prefix collision reports, sample exact collisions when present, and numerical real-phase margin diagnostics.

For an explicit integer phase bank that is not derived from a RoPE base/head-dimension pair:

```bash
python scripts/phase_bank_certify.py --periods 6,9,13,18 --context 128
python scripts/phase_bank_certify.py --preset quantized_shared_factor_256
python scripts/phase_bank_certify.py --preset quantized_lcm_boundary_fail_241
python scripts/phase_bank_certify.py --preset interpolated_x4_boundary_pass_960
python scripts/phase_bank_certify.py --preset interpolated_x4_boundary_fail_961
```

That exact-only path emits theorem-linked discrete certificate fields without a real-phase margin section.

## ML-Engineer Quickstart

The short non-Lean entrypoint is [RoPE Certifier Quickstart](https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/docs/ROPE_CERTIFIER_QUICKSTART.md). It covers installation, presets, custom configs, text and JSON output, theorem ids, and limitations.

## Reproducible Results

The paper sidecar generates the current preset results:

```bash
python sidecars/PAPER_AI_04_ROPE_POSITION_CERTIFIER/python/benchmark_rope_certifier.py --format markdown
```

Committed fixtures:

<p>Preset JSON: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/sidecars/PAPER_AI_04_ROPE_POSITION_CERTIFIER/results/rope_certifier_presets.json">rope_certifier_presets.json</a></p>

<p>Preset Markdown: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/sidecars/PAPER_AI_04_ROPE_POSITION_CERTIFIER/results/rope_certifier_presets.md">rope_certifier_presets.md</a></p>

The model-like presets pass the exact discrete contract under the rounded integer-period phase-bank model. The diagnostic presets include intentional failures and a prefix-pass case so the sample-collision, theorem-backed count, shared-factor, and prefix fields are visible. The exact phase-bank diagnostics include quantized shared-factor cases and interpolation-style scaled-period boundary cases. The sidecar JSON uses compact summaries for readability; the CLI can still emit the full nested certificate. The real-phase rows are numerical diagnostics only.

### Reading The Current Results

The committed sidecar results are meant to be read as contract evidence, not as benchmark wins. A `PASS` in the exact column means the declared integer-period phase bank has no unequal all-channel collision inside the inspected context. A `FAIL` gives a theorem-backed common collision gap and collision count for that declared model.

| Preset | Context | Exact Discrete Contract | Main Evidence | Real-Phase Row |
| --- | ---: | --- | --- | --- |
| `llama_style_10000_4k` | 4096 | PASS | common gap is outside context; first reported prefix already passes at length `5`; the D14 one-channel standard seed covers this request through the 16k proved horizon | numerical scan PASS |
| `llama_style_10000_128k` | 131072 | PASS | common gap is outside context; first reported prefix already passes at length `8`; exact result is still the rounded integer-period model, not a real-valued 128k proof | numerical scan PASS |
| `llama_style_500000_128k` | 131072 | PASS | common gap is outside context; bounded subfamily report finds a sufficient subbank; prefix search does not find a passing prefix in the bounded report; exact result is still the rounded integer-period model | numerical scan PASS |
| `diagnostic_single_channel_10000_20` | 20 | FAIL | common gap `6`; exact integer-bank collision-pair count `24` | numerical scan PASS |
| `diagnostic_two_channel_36_128` | 128 | FAIL | common gap `114`; exact integer-bank collision-pair count `14` | numerical scan PASS |
| `diagnostic_shared_factor_25_64` | 64 | FAIL | common gap `54`; exact integer-bank collision-pair count `10` | numerical scan PASS |

The most important distinction is that the first three rows are exact only after the real-valued RoPE schedule has been converted into the declared integer periods used by the certifier. The numerical real-phase scan is reported beside the proof-carrying discrete contract because it is useful engineering evidence, but this page does not call it a proof.

For genuine standard channel 0, the Lean-proved margin currently stops at context `16384`. The exact-rational planner now reports candidate 32k and 64k rows at the same `1/104219` margin and a 128k-frontier first uncovered gap `103993`; those frontier rows are deliberately not theorem cards.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

1. In the exact discrete model, which field tells you whether the first repeated all-channel position gap is inside the inspected context?
2. Why can the `llama_style_10000_128k` row be exact for its declared integer-period bank without proving a real-valued 128k RoPE theorem?
3. In a failure row, what is the difference between a common collision gap and the total integer-bank collision-pair count?

:::

## Real-Phase Proof Front

The exact integer-period contract is already complete enough to use for declared discrete phase banks. Real-valued RoPE needs a different theorem shape because the channel angle usually does not have an exact integer period.

The current real-phase program proves this reduction:

```text
real phase error
-> turn-ratio nearest-integer error
-> positive generated gaps below the context
-> floor and ceiling witnesses for each gap
```

`AIRA-T0041` converts one-channel real phase error into the normalized nearest-integer expression `|gap * alpha - turns|`, where `alpha = frequency / fullTurn`. `AIRA-T0054` says the finite-context predicate is equivalent to checking the generated positive gaps in `List.range context`. `AIRA-T0058` proves that for one fixed real value, checking the integer floor and integer ceiling is equivalent to checking every integer. `AIRA-T0059` applies that result to every generated gap, reducing the remaining integer-turn side of the finite-context margin predicate to two witnesses per gap. `AIRA-T0126` adds the generated-band endpoint bridge: if a whole gap band shares one integer cell and the band endpoints satisfy the lower/upper inequalities, Lean derives interval witnesses for every gap in the band.

That is a real formal improvement, but it is not the final Diophantine theorem. The next mathematical step is to prove, by continued fractions, Diophantine estimates, or an exact finite certificate, that those floor/ceiling inequalities hold for a concrete nonperiodic RoPE turn ratio and margin.

## First Named Finite-Margin Certificate

The first complete certificate on this path is deliberately rational/discretized:

```text
turn ratio = 1 / 4099
context    = 4096
margin     = 1 / 4099
```

Lean proves that every positive gap below `4096` stays at least `1/4099` away from an integer turn for this declared ratio. The reason is finite and exact: the ratio is reduced, and the denominator return gap `4099` is outside the inspected context.

The Python API exposes the same certificate:

```python
from circle_math.applications import certify_rational_preset_4099

certificate = certify_rational_preset_4099()
certificate.pass_certificate      # True
certificate.certified_margin      # 1 / 4099 as a Python float
certificate.theorem_ids           # AIRA-T0056,AIRA-T0059,AIRA-T0060,AIRA-T0061,AIRA-T0062
```

`AIRA-T0060` is the generic proof-carrying certificate interface. `AIRA-T0061` is the named `1/4099` context-4096 finite-margin theorem. `AIRA-T0062` is the one-channel no-near-turn consequence below that certified margin.

This is the first end-to-end theorem-backed certificate shape. It does not certify the standard irrational `1 / (2π)` RoPE channel.

## First Standard RoPE Interval Seed

The theorem-backed certificates for the genuine standard channel are deliberately bounded. The sharpest current 16k named seed is:

```text
turn ratio = 1 / (2π)
context    = 16384
margin     = 1 / 104219
```

Lean proves this by enclosing each inspected value with exact rational bounds. The initial context-7 seed uses:

```text
gap / 8 <= gap / (2π) <= 25*gap / 157
```

for gaps `1` through `6`, using `π <= 4` for the lower side and `3.14 < π` for the sharper upper side.
Later seeds reuse already-certified gaps at smaller advertised margins, then continue with new interval bands. Lean certifies gaps `7` through `43` with:

```text
10*gap / 63 <= gap / (2π) <= 25*gap / 157
```

using `π < 3.15` for the lower side and `3.14 < π` for the upper side, split across integer cells `1` through `6`.
The next obstruction appears at gap `44`, where the older enclosure straddles an integer turn. The current seed crosses it with:

```text
5000*gap / 31416 <= gap / (2π) <= 5000*gap / 31415
```

using `π < 3.1416` and `3.1415 < π`. The context-333 seed uses that same four-decimal enclosure for every gap `1` through `332`, split across generated integer cells `0` through `52`.

The context-710 seed moves to six-decimal bounds:

```text
500000*gap / 3141593 <= gap / (2π) <= 500000*gap / 3141592
```

using `π < 3.141593` and `3.141592 < π`, split across generated cells `0` through `112` for gaps `1` through `709`.

Lean also proves why this exact `1/1024` margin stops there: gap `710` is already within that margin of integer turn `113`. A larger context for channel 0 must therefore use a smaller advertised margin or a different theorem shape; it cannot extend the same lower bound.

The first context-4096 seed lowers the advertised margin and moves to twenty-decimal `π` bounds:

```text
100000000000000000000*gap / 628318530717958647694
  <= gap / (2π)
  <= 100000000000000000000*gap / 628318530717958647692
```

Those exact rational bands now certify gaps `1` through `16383`, split across generated integer cells `0` through `2607`. D9 first certified the conservative margin `1/131072`; D10 reused the same 20-decimal bounds and cell table to certify the tighter margin `1/105000`; D11 tightened the 4k certificate shape to `1/104219`; D12 reached 8k at the weaker margin `1/104220`; D13 reaches 8k at `1/104219`; and D14 reaches 16k at `1/104219`. The current flagship 16k contract is a bracket: Lean proves the `16384`-context channel-0 margin at `1/104219`, and Lean also proves that any margin at or above `1/104218` is impossible for that same context.

D14 is the current proved standard-channel frontier: it keeps the advertised margin at `1/104219` and extends the 20-decimal interval-certificate method to context `16384`. The D14 bridge then turns that channel-0 seed into a conditional one-separating-channel bank certificate when standard channel 0 is present. `AIRA-T0138` packages the 16k bracket: `1/104219` is proved, while every margin at or above `1/104218` is impossible for context `16384`. The gap between those rationals is still unresolved. This is still not a full all-channel margin theorem or a 128k theorem.

Lean also proves sharper negative companions for this seed family. Gap `710` is already within `1/65536`, within `1/104000`, and within `1/104218` of integer turn `113`. That means the D9 `1/131072` margin cannot simply be doubled, the D10 `1/105000` margin cannot simply be raised to `1/104000`, and the D14 `1/104219` margin cannot simply be raised to `1/104218` for any context containing gap `710`. `AIRA-T0118` strengthens that last stop theorem from exactly `1/104218` to every advertised margin at or above it; `AIRA-T0138` packages the positive seed and negative stop as the current 16k margin bracket.

The Python API exposes the theorem-backed seed:

```python
from circle_math.applications import certify_standard_channel0_interval_seed

certificate = certify_standard_channel0_interval_seed()
certificate.pass_certificate      # True
certificate.certified_margin      # 1/104219
certificate.theorem_ids           # AIRA-T0063,AIRA-T0064,AIRA-T0065,...,AIRA-T0138
```

`AIRA-T0063` proves that one rational interval witness covers every integer turn for one gap. `AIRA-T0064` turns a finite table of those witnesses into a finite-context margin. `AIRA-T0077` and `AIRA-T0078` prove that interval witnesses and certificates survive when the advertised margin is decreased. `AIRA-T0065` through `AIRA-T0076` are the context-6, context-7, context-8, and context-19 seeds; `AIRA-T0079` through `AIRA-T0081` are the context-44 seed; `AIRA-T0082` through `AIRA-T0086` are the context-57 seed; `AIRA-T0087` through `AIRA-T0089` are the context-333 seed; `AIRA-T0090` through `AIRA-T0094` are the context-710 seed, using generated six-decimal `π`-bound bands to cover gaps `1` through `709`; `AIRA-T0095` through `AIRA-T0096` prove that the same `1/1024` margin cannot extend past gap `710`; `AIRA-T0097` through `AIRA-T0101` are the conservative context-4096 seed, using generated twenty-decimal `π`-bound bands to cover gaps `1` through `4095`; `AIRA-T0102` is the D9 conditional bank-level transfer; `AIRA-T0103` through `AIRA-T0104` prove that the doubled D9 margin `1/65536` is already blocked by gap `710`; `AIRA-T0105` through `AIRA-T0108` are the tighter D10 context-4096 seed and bank transfer at margin `1/105000`; `AIRA-T0109` through `AIRA-T0110` prove that margin `1/104000` is already blocked by the same gap; `AIRA-T0111` through `AIRA-T0114` are the D11 context-4096 seed and bank transfer at margin `1/104219`; `AIRA-T0115` through `AIRA-T0116` prove that margin `1/104218` is already blocked by gap `710`; `AIRA-T0117` packages the D11 bridge for banks whose first frequency is standard channel 0; `AIRA-T0118` generalizes the obstruction to every margin at or above `1/104218`; `AIRA-T0119` packages the proved/impossible bracket for context `4096`; `AIRA-T0120` through `AIRA-T0122` extend the standard channel-0 interval seed to context `8192` at margin `1/104220`; `AIRA-T0123` through `AIRA-T0124` package the D12 one-separating-channel bank bridge, including the common first-channel bank shape; `AIRA-T0125` packages the weaker 8k margin bracket; `AIRA-T0126` supplies the reusable band-endpoint compression rule for generated interval tables; `AIRA-T0127` through `AIRA-T0132` package the sharper D13 context-8192 seed, one-channel no-near-turn theorem, bank bridges, and margin bracket at `1/104219`; and `AIRA-T0133` through `AIRA-T0138` package the D14 context-16384 seed, one-channel no-near-turn theorem, bank bridges, and margin bracket at the same margin.

This is standard RoPE, but it is still channel-0 based. The D14 bridge can rule out an all-bank near-turn event when standard channel 0 is present and the requested context and margin fit inside the D14 seed; it does not prove independent margins for every channel.

```python
from fractions import Fraction
from circle_math.applications import certify_standard_channel0_d14_bank_request

request = certify_standard_channel0_d14_bank_request(
    requested_context=16384,
    requested_margin=Fraction(1, 104219),
)
request.pass_certificate  # True
request.theorem_ids       # AIRA-T0136,AIRA-T0137
request.tolerance_rule    # tolerance < fullTurn * requestedMargin
```

Larger contexts and full all-channel RoPE-bank margins need generated interval data plus sharper `π`, continued-fraction bounds, or a different proof shape before they can be marked proved.

The Python sidecar now exposes those next targets as candidate plans:

```python
from fractions import Fraction
from circle_math.applications import plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands

d4_plan = plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands(
    pi_bound_preset="d4",
    margin=Fraction(1, 512),
)
d6_plan = plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands(
    pi_bound_preset="d6",
    margin=Fraction(1, 1024),
)
d20_plan = plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands(
    pi_bound_preset="d20",
    margin=Fraction(1, 104219),
)
d20_8k_plan = plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands(
    pi_bound_preset="d20",
    margin=Fraction(1, 104219),
    max_context_length=8192,
)
d20_16k_plan = plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands(
    pi_bound_preset="d20",
    margin=Fraction(1, 104219),
    max_context_length=16384,
)
d20_32k_plan = plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands(
    pi_bound_preset="d20",
    margin=Fraction(1, 104219),
    max_context_length=32768,
)
d20_64k_plan = plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands(
    pi_bound_preset="d20",
    margin=Fraction(1, 104219),
    max_context_length=65536,
)
d20_128k_frontier_plan = plan_standard_channel0_interval_bands(
    pi_bound_preset="d20",
    margin=Fraction(1, 104219),
    max_context_length=131072,
)

d4_plan.theorem_status  # lean_proved_interval_seed_AIRA-T0087_to_AIRA-T0089
d6_plan.context_length  # 710
d6_plan.theorem_status  # lean_proved_interval_seed_AIRA-T0090_to_AIRA-T0094
d20_plan.context_length # 4096
d20_plan.theorem_status # lean_proved_interval_seed_AIRA-T0111_to_AIRA-T0114
d20_8k_plan.context_length # 8192
d20_8k_plan.theorem_status # lean_proved_interval_seed_AIRA-T0127_to_AIRA-T0129
d20_16k_plan.context_length # 16384
d20_16k_plan.theorem_status # lean_proved_interval_seed_AIRA-T0133_to_AIRA-T0135
d20_32k_plan.context_length # 32768
d20_32k_plan.theorem_status # candidate_plan_not_lean_proved
d20_64k_plan.context_length # 65536
d20_64k_plan.theorem_status # candidate_plan_not_lean_proved
d20_128k_frontier_plan.first_uncovered_gap # 103993
```

The d4, d6, conservative d20, tighter d20, sharp 4k d20, weaker 8k d20, sharp 8k d20, and sharp 16k d20 plans are now source tables for compiled Lean declarations. The sidecar now emits a `standard_channel0_frontier_summary`: Lean-proved standard channel-0 margin `1/104219` reaches context `16384`; the exact-rational planner covers `32768` and `65536` at the same margin; and the larger request first fails at gap `103993`. The latter frontier rows are not proofs until matching Lean declarations compile and manifest ids are marked proved. Each emitted band also records the lower endpoint value, upper endpoint value, whether the endpoint cell-margin check passes, and `bridge_theorem_id = AIRA-T0126`.

Read one band like this:

```text
start_gap = 1
end_gap = 6
cell = 0
start_lower_value = 625 / 3927
end_upper_value = 6000 / 6283
endpoint_cell_margin_ok = true
```

This says the generator has one cell band covering gaps `1` through `6`. The lower endpoint already lies at least the requested margin after integer cell `0`, and the upper endpoint still lies at least the requested margin before integer cell `1`. Because multiplication by the rational lower and upper turn-ratio bounds is monotone over nonnegative gaps, `AIRA-T0126` fills in every gap between those endpoints. Future exact-rational plans remain planning artifacts until matching declarations compile and manifest ids are marked proved.

The generated sidecar Markdown and JSON have `Band Endpoint Audit` summaries that sample the first and last band for each standard interval plan. Rerun the Python planner for the complete deterministic band list.

## What To Notice

- Exact collision is not guessed by scanning every pair. The theorem reduces it to divisibility of the position gap.
- If the common exact collision gap is inside the context, the report lists sample colliding position pairs.
- The guaranteed common-gap count is `context - gap`: every counted start gives an all-channel collision at that gap. The LCM theorem upgrades the positive common-gap-multiple sum into the exact total bank collision count for the declared integer-period model. The LCM pass theorem proves no unequal in-context all-channel collision when the LCM reaches the inspected context, and the LCM fail-witness theorem proves an explicit unequal collision exists when the positive LCM is below the context.
- Bounded prefix reports apply the same LCM theorem spine to early channel prefixes, so `first_exact_pass_prefix_length` identifies the first reported prefix whose integer-bank LCM already reaches the inspected context. `AIRA-T0051` proves that appending suffix channels cannot create an unequal full-bank collision after such a prefix already passes.
- Bounded subfamily reports search small selected subbanks; `AIRA-T0052` proves the same no-collision bridge when any contained subbank's LCM reaches the inspected context.
- The per-channel single-period counts are exact for each individual integer period; they are not all-channel bank counts.
- If the exact discrete contract passes, the report still does not claim real model quality or long-context success.
- The real-phase margin scan is useful engineering evidence, but it is not a Lean proof over real-valued trigonometric RoPE.
- The real-valued formal work has begun with an unwrapped phase-gap formula (`AIRA-T0029`), lower-bound precursor (`AIRA-T0030`), one-turn endpoint-error precursor (`AIRA-T0031`), nonnegative full-turn-multiple precursor (`AIRA-T0032`), signed full-turn-multiple precursor (`AIRA-T0033`), turn-separation predicate (`AIRA-T0037`), no-near-turn consequence (`AIRA-T0038`), finite bank-level no-near-turn bridge (`AIRA-T0039`, `AIRA-T0040`), turn-ratio Diophantine scaling bridge (`AIRA-T0041`), finite-context margin consequence (`AIRA-T0042`), bank finite-context margin consequence (`AIRA-T0043`), finite-context monotonicity (`AIRA-T0044`), bank-level certified-context transfer (`AIRA-T0045`), conservative margin downgrade (`AIRA-T0047`), combined context-plus-margin bank transfer (`AIRA-T0050`), integer-turn-ratio and natural-rational-turn-ratio no-positive-margin guardrails (`AIRA-T0053`, `AIRA-T0055`), a positive reduced-rational finite-context certificate before the denominator gap (`AIRA-T0056`), an exact reduced-rational denominator-boundary iff (`AIRA-T0057`), generated-gap finite-enumeration bridge (`AIRA-T0054`), floor/ceiling nearest-integer witness bridge (`AIRA-T0058`, `AIRA-T0059`), the named rational/discretized `1/4099` certificate (`AIRA-T0060` through `AIRA-T0062`), interval-certificate margin monotonicity (`AIRA-T0077`, `AIRA-T0078`), standard channel-0 interval seeds (`AIRA-T0063` through `AIRA-T0094`), the context-710 stop theorem for the older `1/1024` margin (`AIRA-T0095`, `AIRA-T0096`), the conservative context-4096 standard channel-0 interval seed (`AIRA-T0097` through `AIRA-T0101`), the D9 bank transfer (`AIRA-T0102`), the `1/65536` margin obstruction at gap `710` (`AIRA-T0103`, `AIRA-T0104`), the D10 context-4096 standard channel-0 seed (`AIRA-T0105` through `AIRA-T0108`), the `1/104000` margin obstruction at gap `710` (`AIRA-T0109`, `AIRA-T0110`), the D11 context-4096 standard channel-0 seed (`AIRA-T0111` through `AIRA-T0114`), the `1/104218` margin obstruction at gap `710` (`AIRA-T0115`, `AIRA-T0116`), the standard-channel-first bank bridge (`AIRA-T0117`), the generalized at-or-above obstruction (`AIRA-T0118`), the context-4096 margin bracket (`AIRA-T0119`), the weaker context-8192 D12 seed and bridge family (`AIRA-T0120` through `AIRA-T0125`), the generated-band endpoint bridge (`AIRA-T0126`), the sharper context-8192 D13 seed and bridge family (`AIRA-T0127` through `AIRA-T0132`), and the context-16384 D14 seed and bridge family (`AIRA-T0133` through `AIRA-T0138`). The longest proved standard seed is still channel-0 based; it is not a full all-channel, 128k-context, or continued-fraction theorem.

## Theorem Cards

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0021"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0022"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0023"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0024"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0025"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0026"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0027"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0028"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0034"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0035"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0036"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0046"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0048"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0049"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0051"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0052"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0029"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0030"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0031"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0032"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0033"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0037"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0038"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0039"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0040"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0041"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0042"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0043"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0044"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0045"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0047"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0050"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0053"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0054"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0055"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0056"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0057"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0058"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0059"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0126"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0060"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0061"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0062"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0063"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0064"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0065"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0066"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0067"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0068"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0069"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0070"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0071"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0072"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0073"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0074"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0075"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0076"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0077"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0078"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0079"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0080"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0081"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0082"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0083"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0084"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0085"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0086"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0087"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0088"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0089"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0090"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0091"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0092"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0093"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0094"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0095"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0096"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0097"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0098"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0099"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0100"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0101"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0102"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0103"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0104"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0105"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0106"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0107"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0108"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0109"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0110"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0111"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0112"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0113"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0114"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0115"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0116"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0117"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0118"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0119"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0120"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0121"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0122"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0123"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0124"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0125"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0127"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0128"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0129"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0130"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0131"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0132"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0133"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0134"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0135"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0136"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0137"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="AIRA-T0138"></div>

## Dictionary

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0076"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0077"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0078"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0083"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0084"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0080"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0082"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0085"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0086"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0087"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0088"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="COMMON-0089"></div>

## Source Trail

<p data-paper-ref="papers/applications/PAPER_AI_04_ROPE_POSITION_CERTIFIER.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/applications/PAPER_AI_04_ROPE_POSITION_CERTIFIER.md">Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability</a></p>

<p>Lean source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/Circle/Applications/RoPECertifier.lean">Circle/Applications/RoPECertifier.lean</a></p>

<p>Python source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/circle_math/applications/rope_certifier.py">circle_math/applications/rope_certifier.py</a></p>

<p>CLI source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/scripts/rope_certify.py">scripts/rope_certify.py</a></p>

<p>Exact phase-bank CLI source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/scripts/phase_bank_certify.py">scripts/phase_bank_certify.py</a></p>

<div class="book-navigation">
<a href="ai_contract_ladder.qmd">Previous: AI Contract Ladder</a>
<a href="kv_cache_ring_buffer.qmd">Next: KV-Cache Ring Buffer</a>
</div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
<script type="module" src="../../widgets/ai/rope_certifier.js"></script>
